Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 203 upvotes, 34 comments
  2. Lemonade by AMD: a fast and open source local LLM server using GPU and NPU 260 upvotes, 61 comments
  3. Modern SQLite: Features You Didn't Know It Had 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. Email obfuscation: What works in 2026? 277 upvotes, 86 comments
  5. On the trail of ancient art, deep in the Sahara 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  6. Renewables reached nearly 50% of global electricity capacity last year 53 upvotes, 12 comments
  7. Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise LiteLLM 120 upvotes, 35 comments
  8. Quantum computing bombshells that are not April Fools 244 upvotes, 83 comments
  9. Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards Real World Agents 188 upvotes, 67 comments
  10. An Example of Statistical Investigation of the Text Eugene Onegin – Markov, 1913 [pdf] 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  11. EmDash: A Fresh Take on CMS 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  12. Emacs-libgterm: Terminal emulator for Emacs using libghostty-vt 52 upvotes, 15 comments
  13. LinkedIn Is Illegally Searching Your Computer 987 upvotes, 461 comments
  14. Steam on Linux Use Skyrocketed Above 5% in March 634 upvotes, 294 comments
  15. Inside Nepal's Fake Rescue Racket 166 upvotes, 68 comments
  16. Significant Raise of Reports 187 upvotes, 81 comments
  17. Sweden goes back to basics, swapping screens for books in the classroom 514 upvotes, 262 comments
  18. Artemis II will use laser beams to live-stream 4K moon footage at 260 Mbps 101 upvotes, 42 comments
  19. Gone (Almost) Phishin' 132 upvotes, 61 comments
  20. Delve allegedly forked an open-source tool and sold it as its own 109 upvotes, 51 comments
  21. Snow melt-off in American west stuns scientists 26 upvotes, 8 comments
  22. Enabling Codex to Analyze Two Decades of Hacker News Data 53 upvotes, 22 comments
  23. IBM Announces Strategic Collaboration with Arm 218 upvotes, 131 comments
  24. 'Backrooms' and the Rise of the Institutional Gothic 62 upvotes, 31 comments
  25. Artemis computer running two instances of MS outlook; they can't figure out why 101 upvotes, 56 comments
  26. Bringing Clojure programming to Enterprise (2021) 140 upvotes, 88 comments
  27. EmDash – A spiritual successor to WordPress that solves plugin security 649 upvotes, 481 comments
  28. The SpaceX IPO: retail investor notes 79 upvotes, 53 comments
  29. Artemis II Launch Day Updates 1033 upvotes, 900 comments
  30. Telli (YC F24) is hiring engineers, designers, and more (on-site, Berlin) 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 1:00am, 3 April 2026. Source here.
